recommendations: shell_tool_preference violations are STABLE at 71% across 7 cycles with zero improvement under advisory-only rules — mechanical enforcement via a PreToolUse hook blocking ls/sed/cat-as-file-reader as argv[0] is the only lever remaining; all advisory recommendations have been exhausted without effect.

grep_in_compound_bash requires a hook architecture change to scan the full Bash argument string for 'grep' occurrences, not just argv[0]; the current hook is structurally blind to mid-pipeline grep and advisory flagging has produced no change across 3 cycles.

ls-t-for-timestamp-sorted-file-discovery is a confirmed stable anti-pattern (4b1b8967-19 and 4b1b8967-20 in consecutive units of the same session); add an explicit rule directing use of Python glob+stat sort or `find -newer` rather than `ls -t` in shell when the goal is most-recent-file selection.
scope_expansion absent 3 consecutive cycles — no action needed; safe to downgrade from active watch list pending one more clean cycle.
